rationale: In the citing paper, Ding (2007) appears in a grouped citation that supports a general motivation claim that incentive alignment can improve respondent engagement and data quality. The paper does not single out Ding (2007) for a specific theory, mechanism, estimation procedure, or benchmark that is then directly reused. Instead, the citing study presents its own incentive design for consideration decisions, so this reference functions as broad background rather than a distinct substantive link.
